title: Ozothamnus whitei
intro: Ozothamnus whitei, also known as White Ozothamnus, is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to the relief of various health issues such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ozothamnus whitei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ozothamnus whitei include tea infusions, essential oils, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.

The classification of Ozothamnus whitei is also sometimes referred to as Eriochiton whitei.
